data.title: As a review orchestrator, I route and resolve findings without referencing scores
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Update the review workflow orchestration, resolution dispatch, overlay merging, and sprint orchestrator to remove all references to scores, REVIEW_RESULT, and MIN_SCORE.
**Why**: The orchestrator and workflow files consume review output and must be updated to work with the severity-only schema.
**Scope**:
- IN: REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md (remove REVIEW_RESULT parsing, MIN_SCORE references, score-based pass/fail checks), review-fix-dispatch.md (remove "extract scores object" instruction, update resolution loop to use severity), resolve-overlay-findings.sh (remove scores construction from severity mappings, update has_blocking logic), sprint SKILL.md lines 1498 and 1503 (update "all scores >= 4" and "REVIEW_RESULT: passed" references)
- OUT: Infrastructure scripts (Story 1), agent definitions (Story 2), documentation (Story 4)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md does not reference REVIEW_RESULT, MIN_SCORE, or numeric score thresholds for pass/fail determination
  ← Satisfies: "The reviewer agent output contract no longer includes REVIEW_RESULT or MIN_SCORE lines"
- When this story is complete, resolve-overlay-findings.sh derives blocking status from findings[].severity without constructing a scores object
  ← Satisfies: "record-review.sh derives STATUS exclusively from findings[].severity"
- When this story is complete, review-fix-dispatch.md instructs resolution sub-agents to extract the findings array only, with no reference to a scores object
  ← Satisfies: "The reviewer-findings.json schema requires exactly two top-level keys: findings and summary"
- When this story is complete, sprint SKILL.md uses severity-based language instead of "all scores >= 4" and "REVIEW_RESULT: passed"
  ← Satisfies: "All existing severity levels retain their current blocking/non-blocking semantics"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Testing] resolve-overlay-findings.sh is executable code — verify severity-to-blocking mapping preserves fragile as blocking
- [Reliability] review-fix-dispatch.md is an LLM-instruction file — update must be precise to avoid confused sub-agent behavior (scenario analysis flagged this)

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
